Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log with the following columns Case ID Timestamp Activity Resource Region Complexity Steps 1 Calculate the total duration for each case from the first event Submit Claim to the last event Close Claim 2 Identify cases that take significantly longer to complete We can set a threshold eg mean 1 standard deviation or look for outliers 3 Analyze the attributes Resource Region Complexity for these longer cases to find patterns

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets compute the duration for each case

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Actually lets compute in minutes for easier handling Steps to compute duration For each case find the minimum timestamp Submit Claim and the maximum timestamp Close Claim Convert timestamps to datetime objects and compute the difference

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2001 Start 2024 04 01 09 00 End 2024 04 01 10 30 Duration 1 hour 30 minutes 90 minutes Case 2002 Start 2024 04 01 09 05 End 2024 04 02 11 00 Total 1440 115 1555 minutes Case 2003 Start 2024 04 01 09 10 End 2024 04 03 09 30 Total 2880 20 2900 minutes Case 2004 Start 2024 04 01 09 20 End 2024 04 01 10 45 Duration 1 hour 25 minutes 85 minutes Case 2005 Start 2024 04 01 09 25 End 2024 04 04 14 30 Total 4320 305 4625 minutes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now lets list the durations Case 2001 90 minutes Case 2002 1555 minutes Case 2003 2900 minutes Case 2004 85 minutes Case 2005 4625 minutes Clearly cases 2003 and 2005 are taking much longer over two days and 2002 is about one day and a half

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now lets look at the attributes for these long cases Case 2002 Complexity Medium Region B Resources CSR Paul Adjuster Lisa Manager Ann Finance Carl Case 2003 Complexity High Region A Resources CSR Jane Adjuster Mike Manager Bill Finance Alan Case 2005 Complexity High Region B Resources CSR Paul Adjuster Lisa Manager Bill Finance Carl

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Observations 1 Complexity The two longest cases 2003 and 2005 are High complexity and case 2002 is Medium The two shortest 2001 and 2004 are Low complexity

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Observations 2 Region Case 2002 B and 2005 B are in region B and 2003 A is in region A So region B has two long cases and one short 2004 Region A has one long and one short

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Observations 3 Resources For Adjusters Mike case 2001 short case 2003 long Lisa case 2002 long case 2004 short case 2005 long For Managers Ann case 2001 short case 2002 long case 2004 short Bill case 2003 long case 2005 long For Finance Alan case 2001 short case 2003 long Carl case 2002 long case 2004 short case 2005 long

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Also note the activity Request Additional Documents Case 2002 1 request Case 2003 2 requests Case 2005 3 requests Case 2001 0 Case 2004 0

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 1 High complexity cases require more requests for additional documents which leads to longer durations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Indeed Low complexity 0 requests cases 2001 2004 Medium 1 request case 2002 High 2 to 3 requests cases 2003 2005 This pattern is clear

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 2 The number of requests for additional documents is directly related to the duration

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We see Case 2002 1 request 1555 minutes Case 2003 2 requests 2900 minutes Case 2005 3 requests 4625 minutes So the more requests the longer the duration

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Why might that be Each request for additional documents adds a cycle waiting for the customer then re evaluation This can cause significant delays

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Hypothesis 3 Is there a specific region that is slower

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Region A Case 2001 90 minutes low Case 2003 2900 minutes high Region B Case 2002 1555 minutes medium Case 2004 85 minutes low Case 2005 4625 minutes high The high complexity cases in both regions are long The medium in B is long and the low in B is short Without more data we cannot isolate region as the sole factor

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Hypothesis 4 Are there specific resources that are slower

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Adjusters Mike case 2001 short low complexity case 2003 long high complexity 2 requests Lisa case 2002 long medium 1 request case 2004 short low case 2005 long high 3 requests It seems that the adjusters workload complexity and number of requests is the driver Managers and Finance show the same pattern

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Conclusion The primary root cause is the number of requests for additional documents which is heavily influenced by the claim complexity High complexity leads to multiple requests and each request introduces waiting time for the customer to respond and then rework reevaluation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For case 2003 High Region A After the first request at 11 00 on day1 the next request is at 17 00 on the same day Then the approval is on day2 at 16 00 and payment on day3 at 09 00 For case 2005 High Region B First request 2024 04 01 11 30 Then 2024 04 02 17 00 Then 2024 04 03 15 00 Then approval 2024 04 04 10 00 The delays between requests are long likely waiting for customer response

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Proposed Explanations 1 High complexity claims require more additional documents leading to multiple cycles and long waiting times 2 The waiting time for customer response is a major contributor to the overall duration

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Suggestions 1 Proactively request all necessary documents at the time of submission for high complexity claims 2 Set clear expectations with the customer about the documents needed and the time they have to respond 3 For high complexity claims assign a dedicated resource case manager to handle the communication and document collection 4 Monitor the time taken for customer response and if it exceeds a threshold escalate or send reminders 5 Analyze the types of documents frequently requested and ensure that the initial submission form captures as much as possible

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: For region specific performance Case 2005 Region B High took the longest It might be that Region B has fewer resources or higher workload We do not have data on the workload of each resource but it might be that adjusters in Region B are overloaded

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Suggestion Balance the workload among adjusters especially in Region B Consider transferring some cases to Region A if they have capacity

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: For resources Manager Bill handled the two longest cases 2003 and 2005 Maybe he is slower Or maybe he is assigned to high complexity cases by design Since the long cases are due to many requests it might be that Manager Bill is assigned to the most complex cases

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Suggestion Provide additional training or support to adjusters and managers handling high complexity cases Consider having a specialized team for high complexity claims

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We can create a table of cases with their durations and attributes to summarize Case ID Duration minutes Complexity Region Adjuster Requests 2001 90 Low A Mike 0 2002 1555 Medium B Lisa 1 2003 2900 High A Mike 2 2004 85 Low B Lisa 0 2005 4625 High B Lisa 3 The correlation between the number of requests and duration is clear Therefore the root cause of long case durations is primarily the number of requests for additional documents which is driven by claim complexity The other attributes Region Resource do not show a consistent pattern without considering complexity and requests
